steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify corresponding sides from the vertex correspondence
steps.content: Since A corresponds to D and B corresponds to E, side $\overline{AB}$ corresponds to side $\overline{DE}$. Since B corresponds to E and C corresponds to F, side $\overline{BC}$ corresponds to side $\overline{EF}$.

steps.title: Calculate the scale factor
steps.content:
For similar triangles, the ratio of corresponding sides is constant. Find the scale factor from triangle ABC to triangle DEF using the known corresponding sides:
$$\text{Scale factor} = \frac{DE}{AB} = \frac{12}{9} = \frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to find EF
steps.content:
Since $\overline{BC}$ and $\overline{EF}$ are corresponding sides, they must have the same ratio as all other corresponding sides:
$$\frac{EF}{BC} = \frac{4}{3}$$
$$EF = 30 \times \frac{4}{3} = \frac{120}{3} = 40$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
